rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF BURTON J. GOLDSTEIN
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #5, Report, Fees, Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Appearance required. Issues to include: 1) Petitioner alleges that he received compensation from the estate in the amount of $200,000 in 2005 without a court order, based upon advice of his then counsel, Theodore Kolb, and the Estate's tax attorney, Leslie Pesterfield, in order to avoid a large tax liability. Petitioner asserts that the beneficiaries have waived any objections, as they have waived any accounting. He also alleges that he would be entitled to a minimum of $188,000 for ordinary services based upon $25M in assets coming into the estate, and he waives the right to any additional fees. 2) Petitioner requests reimbursement from the estate $200,000 for out of pocket expenses. Petitioner alleges that he has spent over $790k in expenses, but that he only seeks $200k. He alleges that he paid attorney's fees and costs, including to indemnify a law firm, which was a condition for the payment of $24M to this estate. Petitioner cites Probate Code 10553 for authority of the personal representative to incur costs in defending the estate, but does not address relevant Local Rules, including 14.59. Remote appearances should be made whenever possible. Please review the 9th amended general order. The following information pertains to appearances by Zoom: WHEN ENTERING "YOUR NAME" ON ZOOM, BOTH THE FIRST AND LAST NAME, AS WELL THE CASE NUMBER, MUST BE INCLUDED. Meeting ID: 875 8017 7925 Password: 722809 To appear by telephone through Zoom, call 1-669-900-6833, and follow the prompts, using the meeting ID and password above. Information regarding appearance by CourtCall can be found in the 9th amended general order.

rulings.Case Title: IN RE: STELLA CEAN YI LEUNG 2019 IRREVOCABLE
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #1, Report, Fees
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 3/30/2021. Issues: 1) The amended account alleges the starting value of the account is $500,000.00. The I&A not appraised by a referee alleges $498,492.34 assets at the beginning of the account. The court cannot determine the starting value of the account. Petitioner's have not filed a request for appointment of probate referee. [LRSF 14.12]. A petition filed pursuant to Probate Code 8903 must state the source of the values included in the Inventory and Appraisal. Waivers of appraisal are not favored and are not routinely granted. [LRSF 14.27].

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE IRREVOCABLE TRUST AGREEMENT DATED MARCH 26, 1981 TRUST
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petition For Order (I)Approving Appointment Of Successor Co-Trustee, (Ii)Approving Trustee Succession Plan, (Iii)Determining Construction Of Trust Ins
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 3/30/2021. Current Issues: 1) Notice must be given on mantaory judicial council form DE-115. 2) The Court will require a bond of all successor trustees not named in the trust instrument unless all beneficiaries waive bond. The assets on hand must be listed with the fair market value to allow the Court to set the bond. [LRSF 14.6 G.] 3) Please provide legal authority for whether a GAL needs to be appointed for the minor contingent beneficaries.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF THOMAS FOON LEE
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
rulings.Rulings: Grant without hearing, although the optional JC form "Declaration" should not be used to supplement or amend a petition. Supplements or amendments to Probate petitions must be prepared on pleading paper. Status of closing the estate is set for 8/23/22. If a petition for final distribution or a report of status with a petition requesting additional time for estate administration is not filed by 8/12/22, an appearance will be required on 8/23/22.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF HAROLD LEVINE
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 03/24/2021. Issues: 1) A proposed nonresident personal representative will be required to post a bond to protect California creditors, even if the will waives, or all beneficiaries waive, bond. A declaration or attachment to the petition setting forth in detail the anticipated liabilities of the decedent and claims against the estate will be used by the Court to determine the amount of the bond, but in no event will the bond be less than $20,000. [LRSF 14.16 F.] 2) DE-147S appears to be filed but is not yet viewable by Examiner. This issue may be removed when the document is viewable by Examiner.
